steps.instruction: Heat an aliquot of Solution CD6 to 60°C in a dry-bath. Pipette 110 µL per sample into a microcentrifuge tube. This will create a buffer.
Label PowerBead tubes with the sample ID.
Spin the PowerBead Tube briefly to pull beads to the bottom of the tube and add approximately 0.25 g ( 250 mg ) of sample.
Prep the scale area: place weighing paper down on the scale, place 10 mL beaker on the weighing paper, and place PowerBead tube inside the beaker. Tare the scale.
Using a disposable spatula, place 0.25 g of sample into the tube.
Record the weight.
Avoid cross-contamination. Clean up any spills in between each sample and change gloves if needed.
Add 800 µL of Solution CD1 to the filled PowerBead tubes. Vortex briefly to mix.
Secure the PowerBead tube horizontally on a Vortex Adapter at max speed for 00:10:00 . If there are more than 12 samples simultaneously, increase the vortexing time by 5-10 minutes.
While the PowerBead tubes are mixing you can begin to prep and label your tubes for the next steps.
Centrifuge the PowerBead tube at 15000 x g, °C, 1 minute .
Transfer the supernatant to a labeled, clean 2 mL microcentrifuge tube (provided in kit). Expect 500 µL - 600 µL . The supernatant may still contain some sample particles.
Add 200 µL of Solution CD2 and vortex for 00:00:05 . CD2 is stored at 4°C and should be put back when done with it.
Centrifuge at 15000 x g, Room temperature, 1 minute . Avoiding the pellet, transfer up to 700 µL of supernatant to a clean 2 mL microcentrifuge tube (provided in kit). Expect 500 µL - 600 µL .
Add 600 µL Solution CD3 and vortex for 00:00:05 .
Load 650 µL of the lysate onto a labeled MB Spin Column and centrifuge at 15000 x g, °C, 1 minute .
Discard the flow-through and repeat Step 12 to ensure that all the lysate has passed through the MB Spin Column.
Carefully place the MB Spin Column into a labeled, clean 2 mL Collection Tube (provided in kit). Avoid splashing any flow-through onto the MB Spin Column.
Add 500 µL of Solution EA to the MB Spin Column. Centrifuge at 15000 x g, °C, 1 minute .
Discard the flow-through and place the MB Spin Column back into the same 2 mL Collection Tube .
Add 500 µL of Solution C5 to the MB Spin Column . Centrifuge at 15000 x g, °C, 1 minute .
Discard the flow-through and place the MB Spin Column into a new 2 mL Collection Tube (provided in kit).
Centrifuge at 16000 x g, °C, 2 minutes . Carefully place the MB Spin Column into a labeled, 1.5 mL Elution Tube (provided in kit).
Add 100 µL of the 60 °C Solution CD6 to the center of the white filter membrane.
Centrifuge at 15000 x g, °C, 1 minute . Discard the MB Spin Column . The DNA is now ready for downstream applications.

Optional: Transfer all the DNA in each tube into a labeled 0.5 mL Lo-Bind Tube. Use the sample’s full name. Include the date on the side of the tube. Write legibly and keep the samples in chronological order. Make sure the caps are all the way snapped and closed to prevent evaporation.

Next the DNA can continue to Procedure DNA Quantification go to step #24 OR the DNA should be stored in a labeled box at -20 °C . DNA Isolation creates a Stock DNA of sample that is stored. Prior to PCR or qPCR, this Stock DNA will be aliquoted to create Working Stock DNA . The Working Stock DNA will be at a concentration that will allow for the 5 µL of sample required in the PCR recipes. See SOP: IDT Primer & gBlock Hydration and Aliquots.
Using the Nanodrop One System machine - From the nanodrop home screen, select the Nucleic Acids application tab, then the dsDNA application. Follow the on-screen instructions.
Lift the instrument arm and clean the upper and lower pedestals with a new laboratory wipe.
Measure a blank:
Pipette 2 µL buffer solution (Solution CD6 from the PowerSoil Pro Kit) onto the lower pedestal and lower the arm.
Wait for the measurement to complete.
Lift the arm and clean both pedestals with a new laboratory wipe.
Measure the samples:
Gently mix the DNA sample briefly. Keep the samples cool ( On ice or 4 °C refrigeration) throughout this process to avoid degradation.
Pipette 2 µL sample onto the lower pedestal and lower the arm.
Wait for the measurement to complete.

Record the measurement for nanodrop (ng/µL), A260/280, & A260/230. A260/280 values greater than or equal to 1.8 (DNA) to 2.1 (RNA), and A260/230 values around 2.0-2.2 are suitable for further analysis. A lower ratio indicates the sample contains protein or organic contaminants.
Lift the arm and clean both pedestals with a new laboratory wipe.
Repeat Step 28 until all the samples have been analyzed. When finished, tap ‘End Experiment’.
Lift the arm and clean both pedestals with a new wipe.
